name: Gloucester County School Board v. G.G.
question:
1. Should an unpublished agency letter which interprets the agency’s own regulation be accorded deference?
  2. In this case, should the Department of Education’s interpretation of Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972 and its regulation permitting separation of restrooms on the basis of sex be accorded deference?
description: A case in which the Court will decide whether an unpublished agency letter which interprets the agency’s own regulation be accorded deference and whether the Department of Education’s interpretation of Title IX and its regulation permitting separation of restrooms on the basis of sex be accorded deference.
